# GitHub repo metadata (applied)

**Applied to `JorgeEd13/forge-pdm-mlops` on 2026-06-25** via `gh repo edit` (the
description + the 14 topics below are live in the **About** sidebar). Kept here so
they stay in sync with the README tagline and are re-appliable. Re-run the command
to update them if the tagline changes.

## Description (the "About" one-liner)

> MLOps pipeline over synthetic predictive-maintenance telemetry: MLflow tracking + model registry, FastAPI serving, and a drift → auto-retrain loop (Evidently + Prefect). Clean-room, 100% synthetic.

## Topics (tags)

```
mlops, mlflow, model-registry, drift-detection, predictive-maintenance,
fastapi, prefect, evidently, machine-learning, synthetic-data,
lightgbm, scikit-learn, ci-cd, python
```

## Apply with the gh CLI (after the repo exists)

```bash
# from the repo root, once the GitHub remote exists:
gh repo edit \
  --description "MLOps pipeline over synthetic predictive-maintenance telemetry: MLflow tracking + model registry, FastAPI serving, and a drift → auto-retrain loop (Evidently + Prefect). Clean-room, 100% synthetic." \
  --add-topic mlops \
  --add-topic mlflow \
  --add-topic model-registry \
  --add-topic drift-detection \
  --add-topic predictive-maintenance \
  --add-topic fastapi \
  --add-topic prefect \
  --add-topic evidently \
  --add-topic machine-learning \
  --add-topic synthetic-data \
  --add-topic lightgbm \
  --add-topic scikit-learn \
  --add-topic ci-cd \
  --add-topic python
```

> Note: GitHub allows up to 20 topics; these 14 are the high-signal set for a
> recruiter/founder scan. The `homepage` field can point at the live `/health`
> link once F6 (hosted deploy) ships.
